title: NeatContext pitches local team knowledge as the missing layer for better LLM answers
friendlyTitle: NeatContext helps AI use your team's own knowledge
summary: NeatContext is a local-first desktop workspace that connects team profiles, local folders, runbooks, and read-only MCP tools to the LLMs a user already has. Its examples focus on operational troubleshooting where internal context changes the answer from a generic checklist to a specific safe action.
friendlySummary: NeatContext lets an AI assistant look at local team notes, runbooks, and safe read-only tools. The goal is to give answers based on your real work context, not generic advice.
whyItMatters: Many AI failures at work are context failures rather than model failures. Local-first context tools could help teams get more useful answers without copying all institutional knowledge into another hosted system.
friendlyWhyItMatters: AI is more useful when it understands your team's rules and history. A local-first tool may also feel safer for private company knowledge.
